R Notebook

library(data.table)
library(Rtsne)
library(R.utils)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggcorrplot)

Processing data

Sade-Feldman(2018) data

fnm1 = "GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_nolabel_1.txt.gz"
fnm2 = "GSE120575_Sade_Feldman_melanoma_single_cells_2.txt.gz"

sf_tpm1 = fread(paste0("../../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/", fnm1), fill = TRUE, header = TRUE)

sf_tpm2 = fread(paste0("../../scRNAseq/Sade_Feldman_2018/", fnm2), fill = TRUE, drop = 16293, col.names = colnames(sf_tpm1))

ls_tpm = list(sf_tpm1,sf_tpm2)
sf_tpm = rbindlist(ls_tpm)

rm(ls_tpm)
rm(sf_tpm1)
rm(sf_tpm2)
dim(sf_tpm) # 55737 x 16292
## [1] 55737 16292
gene.names = sf_tpm$V1
rownames(sf_tpm) = gene.names
sf_tpm$V1 = NULL

number of cells that each gene is expressed in, to select genes that have expression in a certain amount of cells

gene.grouping = rep(1:28, each = 2000)[1:dim(sf_tpm)[1]]
cells.per.gene = NULL
for (i in 1:28) {
  temp.counts = rowSums(sf_tpm[gene.grouping==i,] > 0)
  cells.per.gene = c(cells.per.gene,temp.counts)
}
large.express.genes.all = gene.names[which(cells.per.gene > 500)] # kept 12705 genes
rm(gene.grouping)

11 cell types we defined from Sade-Feldman(2018) data

cell_type = read.table("./output/cell_type.txt",header = TRUE,sep = "\t")
table(cell_type$type)
## 
##               B cells       CD4 T.mem cells       CD4 T.reg cells 
##                  1421                  1099                  1313 
##           CD8_B cells           CD8_G cells       Dendritic cells 
##                  2482                  3043                   275 
## Monocytes/Macrophages             NK1 cells             NK2 cells 
##                  1341                   463                  1903 
##             NK3 cells          Plasma cells           unclustered 
##                  1147                   290                  1514
clustered_cells = cell_type$cell[cell_type$type != "unclustered"]
# select TPM data for 12705 genes with large expression in 14777 grouped cells
clustered_tpm_LEgenes = sf_tpm[rownames(sf_tpm) %in% large.express.genes.all, ..clustered_cells] 
rownames(clustered_tpm_LEgenes) = large.express.genes.all
rm(sf_tpm)

percentages of zero expression genes for each cell in each cell type

# calculate the percentages of genes with zero expression for each cell
zeros.per.cell = colSums(clustered_tpm_LEgenes==0) / nrow(clustered_tpm_LEgenes)
zero.percentages = data.frame(percentage.of.zero = zeros.per.cell, grouping = factor(cell_type$type[match(clustered_cells, cell_type$cell)]))
head(zero.percentages)
# distribution of the percentages of zero expression genes for cells by cell type
ggplot(data = zero.percentages) + 
  geom_histogram(aes(x=percentage.of.zero), binwidth = 0.01) + 
  facet_wrap(~grouping) + 
  ggtitle("Distribution of the percentages of zero expression genes")

rm(zeros.per.cell)

Based on the graph, the spread of distribution of the percentages of zero expression genes is quite different for each cell type, so I didn’t use a single number as the cutoff to choose which cells to keep, instead, I select 100 cells with the smallest percentages of zero expression genes.

fewzero_cells = unname(unlist(by(zero.percentages, zero.percentages$grouping, FUN = function(x) rownames(x)[order(x$percentage.of.zero)][1:100])))

LM22 signature matrix

  • select genes that appear both in the LM22 signature matrix and the SF data
  • in those genes, further select genes that have expression in a relatively large amount of cells
CBST_LM22 = read.table("../../_reference/LM22.txt", sep = "\t", header = TRUE, as.is = TRUE)
head(CBST_LM22)
CBST.SFgenes = CBST_LM22$Gene.symbol[CBST_LM22$Gene.symbol%in%gene.names] # 538 out of 547 
CBST.largeExpression.genes = CBST.SFgenes[CBST.SFgenes %in% large.express.genes.all] # 323 out of 538
CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22 = CBST_LM22[CBST_LM22$Gene.symbol%in%CBST.largeExpression.genes,] # 323 x 23
head(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22)

Compare the correlation of cell types

  • Compare the correlation between our 11 cell types.
  • Compare the correlation between the cell types in the LM22 vs. the 11 cell types we had.
  • Two types of correlation: using all cells for each cell type to calculate mean gene expression, or calculate the mean expression using 100 cells with smallest percentages of zero expression genes.

Correlations between our 11 cell types

using mean expression of large expression genes shared between SF data and LM22 matrix

LEgenes_CBST_tpm = as.data.table(t(clustered_tpm_LEgenes[large.express.genes.all %in% CBST.largeExpression.genes,]))
rownames(LEgenes_CBST_tpm) = clustered_cells
colnames(LEgenes_CBST_tpm) = CBST.largeExpression.genes
LEgenes_CBST_tpm[1:5,1:5]
LEgenes_CBST_tpm$grouping = factor(cell_type$type[match(row.names(LEgenes_CBST_tpm), cell_type$cell)])
table(LEgenes_CBST_tpm$grouping)
## 
##               B cells       CD4 T.mem cells       CD4 T.reg cells 
##                  1421                  1099                  1313 
##           CD8_B cells           CD8_G cells       Dendritic cells 
##                  2482                  3043                   275 
## Monocytes/Macrophages             NK1 cells             NK2 cells 
##                  1341                   463                  1903 
##             NK3 cells          Plasma cells 
##                  1147                   290
LEgene_CBST_expression = LEgenes_CBST_tpm[, lapply(.SD, mean), by = grouping] # 11 x 324
LEgene_CBST_expression = LEgene_CBST_expression[order(LEgene_CBST_expression$grouping),]
LEgene_CBST_expression[,1:6]
LEgene_CBST_compare_expression = as.data.frame(t(LEgene_CBST_expression[,-1]))
colnames(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression) = LEgene_CBST_expression$grouping
head(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression)
rm(LEgene_CBST_expression)
rm(LEgenes_CBST_tpm)

The patterns of the correlation roughly match our expectation: - Plasma cells, Monocytes/Macrophages, Dendritic cells and B cells do no have major correlation with other cell types, matches the clustering result that these 4 cell types form most distinct clusters - The 3 NK cell clusters are closely related with each other - CD8 cells, NK cells, and CD4 T cells are closely related

cormat_LE.CBSTgenes_11type = cor(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression, method = "spearman")
ggcorrplot(cormat_LE.CBSTgenes_11type,type = "upper")

using mean expression for large expression genes shared between SF data and LM22 matrix over cells with fewer zero gene expression

LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm = as.data.table(t(clustered_tpm_LEgenes[large.express.genes.all %in% CBST.largeExpression.genes, ..fewzero_cells])) 
rownames(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm) = fewzero_cells
colnames(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm) = CBST.largeExpression.genes
LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm[1:5,1:5]
LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm$grouping = factor(cell_type$type[match(row.names(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm), cell_type$cell)])
table(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm$grouping)
## 
##               B cells       CD4 T.mem cells       CD4 T.reg cells 
##                   100                   100                   100 
##           CD8_B cells           CD8_G cells       Dendritic cells 
##                   100                   100                   100 
## Monocytes/Macrophages             NK1 cells             NK2 cells 
##                   100                   100                   100 
##             NK3 cells          Plasma cells 
##                   100                   100
LEgene_CBST_few0_expression = LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm[, lapply(.SD, mean), by = grouping] # 11 x 324
LEgene_CBST_few0_expression[,1:6]
LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression = as.data.frame(t(LEgene_CBST_few0_expression[,-1]))
colnames(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression) = LEgene_CBST_few0_expression$grouping
head(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression)
rm(LEgene_CBST_few0_expression)
rm(LEgenes_CBST_few0_tpm)

The result using cells with fewer zero expression genes is not very different from the result using all cells.

cormat_LE.CBST.few0genes_11type = cor(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression, method = "spearman")
ggcorrplot(cormat_LE.CBST.few0genes_11type,type = "upper")

Correlations between our 11 cell types and the cell types in the LM22 matrix

using mean expression of large expression genes shared between SF data and LM22 matrix

cormat_LM22_11type = matrix(0,nrow = 22, ncol = 11)
colnames(cormat_LM22_11type)= colnames(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression)
rownames(cormat_LM22_11type) = colnames(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22)[-1]
for (i in 1:22) {
  for (j in 1:11) {
   cormat_LM22_11type[i,j] = cor(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_compare_expression[,j]), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22[,i+1]),method = "spearman")
  }
}

The correlations between our 11 cell types and the cell types in the LM22 matrix still have some unexpected patterns

p = ggcorrplot(t(cormat_LM22_11type), tl.cex = 6)
p + scale_fill_gradient2(limit = range(cormat_LM22_11type), low = "blue", high =  "red", mid = "white", midpoint = mean(range(cormat_LM22_11type)))

using mean expression for large expression genes shared between SF data and LM22 matrix over cells with fewer zero gene expression

cormat_LM22_11type_few0 = matrix(0,nrow = 22, ncol = 11)
colnames(cormat_LM22_11type_few0)= colnames(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression)
rownames(cormat_LM22_11type_few0) = colnames(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22)[-1]
for (i in 1:22) {
  for (j in 1:11) {
   cormat_LM22_11type_few0[i,j] = cor(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression[,j]), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22[,i+1]),method = "spearman")
  }
}

The result using cells with fewer zero expression genes is not very different from the result using all cells. But we observed some potential issues: LM22 B cells seem to be correlated with all other cell types except B cells in our data.

p = ggcorrplot(t(cormat_LM22_11type_few0), tl.cex = 6)
p + scale_fill_gradient2(limit = range(cormat_LM22_11type_few0), low = "blue", high =  "red", mid = "white", midpoint = mean(range(cormat_LM22_11type_few0)))

Compare the scatterplot of gene expressions between LM22 cell types and our cell types

B cells and B cells

par(mar=c(3,3,2.5,0.8), mgp=c(1.5,0.5,0), cex.axis=0.85, cex.lab=0.85, cex.main=0.85)
layout(matrix(c(1,2,3,4),nrow=2,ncol = 2,byrow = TRUE))

scatter.smooth(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression$`B cells`), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.naive), main = "Our B cell v.s. LM 22 naive B cells", xlab = "our B cell", ylab = "LM 22 naive B cells", pch=20, cex = 0.5, lpars = list(col="red"))

scatter.smooth(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression$`B cells`), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.memory), main = "Our B cell v.s. LM 22 memory B cells", xlab = "our B cell", ylab = "LM 22 memory B cells", pch=20, cex = 0.5, lpars = list(col="red"))

B cells and CD8 cells

par(mar=c(3,3,2.5,0.8), mgp=c(1.5,0.5,0), cex.axis=0.85, cex.lab=0.85, cex.main=0.85)
layout(matrix(c(1,2,3,4),nrow=2,ncol = 2,byrow = TRUE))

scatter.smooth(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression$`CD8_B cells`), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.naive), main = "Our CD8_B cell v.s. LM 22 naive B cells", xlab = "our CD8_B cell", ylab = "LM 22 naive B cells", pch=20, cex = 0.5, lpars = list(col="red"))

scatter.smooth(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression$`CD8_B cells`), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.memory), main = "Our CD8_B cell v.s. LM 22 memory B cells", xlab = "our CD8_B cell", ylab = "LM 22 memory B cells", pch=20, cex = 0.5, lpars = list(col="red"))

scatter.smooth(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression$`CD8_G cells`), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.naive), main = "Our CD8_G cell v.s. LM 22 naive B cells", xlab = "our CD8_G cell", ylab = "LM 22 naive B cells", pch=20, cex = 0.5, lpars = list(col="red"))

scatter.smooth(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression$`CD8_B cells`), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$B.cells.memory), main = "Our CD8_G cell v.s. LM 22 memory B cells", xlab = "our CD8_G cell", ylab = "LM 22 memory B cells", pch=20, cex = 0.5, lpars = list(col="red"))

CD8 cells and CD8 cells

par(mar=c(3,3,2.5,0.8), mgp=c(1.5,0.5,0), cex.axis=0.85, cex.lab=0.85, cex.main=0.85)
layout(matrix(c(1,2,3,4),nrow=2,ncol = 2,byrow = TRUE))

scatter.smooth(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression$`CD8_B cells`), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$T.cells.CD8), main = "Our CD8_B cell v.s. LM 22 CD8 T cells", xlab = "our CD8_B cell", ylab = "LM 22 CD8 T cells", pch=20, cex = 0.5, lpars = list(col="red"))

scatter.smooth(as.numeric(LEgene_CBST_few0_compare_expression$`CD8_G cells`), as.numeric(CBST_SFLEgenes_LM22$T.cells.CD8), main = "Our CD8_g cell v.s. LM 22 CD8 T cells", xlab = "our CD8_G cell", ylab = "LM 22 CD8 T cells", pch=20, cex = 0.5, lpars = list(col="red"))

gc()
